name: Focus Owns Input
designPattern: Pane-Scoped Input Ownership / Shell-Managed Input Stack
requirements: Tests ARE the Spec roadmap principle

userStory.asA: builder of a multi-pane framed Bijou app
userStory.iWant: the focused pane to own local keyboard and mouse input
userStory.soThat: focus, help, and interaction routing all describe the same truth instead of leaving inactive panes responsive

acceptanceCriteria: A framed page can declare pane-scoped input areas keyed by `paneId` without replacing the existing page-global API surface
When focus moves to a different pane, only that pane's local key bindings remain active
Inactive panes do not continue to react to local keys after focus has left them
Existing shell modal layers such as help and command palette still preempt pane-local input
Existing page modal capture via `modalKeyMap` still preempts pane-local input
Frame shell bindings such as pane cycling and help/palette toggles remain coherent while pane-local input exists
Pane-local mouse handling can run for the focused or targeted pane after shell chrome and shell overlays intercept first
The full help surface can reflect the focused pane's bindings instead of only page-global bindings
The DOGFOOD docs shell can move focus between lanes without arrow keys continuing to mutate the family lane when another pane is active
backwardCompatibility: Existing `FramePage.keyMap`, `modalKeyMap`, `globalKeys`, and page update behavior remain supported. Pane-scoped input is additive in v0, with the new layer treated as page-derived routing rather than a breaking shell rewrite.

inScope: DOGFOOD docs shell as the proving surface
outScope: General nested input graphs beyond framed panes
A full public input-stack authoring API for framed apps
Standardizing every interactive component around this model in the same slice

testPlan.goldenPath: Write failing app-frame tests that prove pane-local keymaps only fire for the focused pane, that focus changes replace the active layer, and that help/palette/modals still preempt lower layers. Then migrate the DOGFOOD docs shell so focusing the variants pane stops arrow keys from moving the family lane.
edgeCases: Focus changes via keyboard pane cycling
Focus changes via mouse click and mouse wheel
A page with no focused pane-local input area
A page modal open while a pane-local keymap exists
A shell modal open while a pane-local keymap exists
testPlan.fuzzStress: Not a priority in v0. The core risk is incorrect routing precedence, not large-scale throughput.
failureCases: The focused pane highlight changes but inactive pane bindings still fire
Pane-local bindings can suppress help or command-palette capture incorrectly
Mouse focus and keyboard focus diverge and activate different panes
The help surface still shows stale page-global bindings after focus changes
The docs shell requires bespoke hacks instead of using the new frame capability

estimatedComplexity.linesOfCode: ~200-350 lines across app-frame routing, help integration, and docs-shell migration, plus targeted regressions
estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(L) per key event where L is the number of active shell-derived layers; v0 should keep this small and bounded
